root_cause: The deployed systemd unit set ProtectHome=read-only but made only the repository out directory writable. Codex CLI 0.144.6 needs its own state directory during startup even for --ephemeral jobs, so both Luna and the newly wired Sol fallback exited before emitting JSONL.

diagnostic_evidence.before: codex doctor under the deployed hardening emitted a read-only runtime-path warning
diagnostic_evidence.after: the warning disappeared when only /home/dev/.codex was added to ReadWritePaths

fixes: Keep ProtectHome=read-only and add only %h/.codex to systemd ReadWritePaths.
Retry the configured gpt-5.6-sol understand fallback once after a public Luna runtime failure.
Extract and log only allowlisted upstream type, code, status, or process-exit classification.
regression_tests: public Luna nonzero retries Sol with the same public ephemeral policy
public nonzero diagnostics exclude prompts, stderr, and upstream messages
the deployed unit preserves ProtectHome=read-only while granting the Codex state path
